questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of CONUNDRUM:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: Earthquakes occur when tectonic plates shift suddenly. Q: The point of origin is called the epicentre. R: Seismic waves travel through the Earth's crust. S: The Richter scale measures the magnitude of earthquakes.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of AUDACIOUS:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: Coral reefs are among the most diverse ecosystems. Q: They provide habitat for numerous marine species. R: Climate change and ocean acidification threaten their survival. S: Conservation programmes are working to protect and restore reefs.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of PRUDENT:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: Inflation reduces the purchasing power of money. Q: Central banks adjust interest rates to control inflation. R: High inflation can erode savings and reduce living standards. S: Moderate inflation is considered healthy for economic growth.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of FACILITATE:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: The Green Revolution increased agricultural productivity. Q: High-yield crop varieties were developed and introduced. R: Chemical fertilisers and pesticides were used extensively. S: This helped many countries achieve food security.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of PERILOUS:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: Open-pit mining is used when coal seams are near the surface. Q: The topsoil and overburden are removed first. R: The coal is then extracted using heavy machinery. S: After mining, the land is reclaimed for other uses.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of ABUNDANT:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: Cyber security is a growing concern for organisations. Q: Data breaches can result in significant financial losses. R: Companies invest in firewalls and encryption technologies. S: Employee training is essential to prevent phishing attacks.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of HARMONY:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: The Industrial Revolution transformed manufacturing processes. Q: It began in England in the late 18th century. R: Steam engines powered factories and railways. S: This led to unprecedented economic growth.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of ARDENT:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: Water scarcity is affecting many regions of India. Q: Groundwater levels are depleting at alarming rates. R: Efficient irrigation techniques can help conserve water. S: Rainwater harvesting is also being promoted.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of PERIPHERAL:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: He has been working here since five years.
options: He has been working here for five years.
He has worked here since five years.
He worked here since five years.
He is working here since five years.
questions.solution: He has been working here for five years.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: DC motor back EMF: E_b = V - I_a·R_a
questions.solution: At start: E_b=0, I_a=V/R_a (high). Starter limits current.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Illuminance (E):
questions.solution: E = Φ/A. 1 lux = 1 lm/m². Daylight: 10,000-100,000 lux. Office: 300-500 lux.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which statement about skin effect is correct?
options: Effective AC resistance of a conductor increases with frequency.
It makes current flow at the conductor centre.
It reduces resistance at high frequency.
questions.solution: Skin effect crowds AC current toward the surface, reducing the effective area and raising AC resistance with frequency.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Routh–Hurwitz criterion is used to determine:
options: The number of closed-loop poles in the right half-plane
questions.solution: Sign changes in the first column of the Routh array count the RHP poles (unstable roots).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The time constant of a series RL circuit is:
questions.solution: τ = L/R; the current reaches ~63% of its final value in one time constant.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Regarding a synchronous motor, which statement is INCORRECT?
options: Over-excitation makes it draw leading current.
It runs at exactly synchronous speed in steady state.
It can be used to improve system power factor.
It is inherently self-starting from rest.
questions.solution: A synchronous motor produces no net starting torque at standstill, so it is not self-starting.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Voltage regulation:
questions.solution: Positive for lagging PF. Negative for leading PF (Ferranti effect).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a single line-to-ground fault, the three sequence networks are connected in:
questions.solution: An LG fault connects positive, negative and zero sequence networks in series.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Stability (Routh-Hurwitz):
options: All poles in left half of s-plane
questions.solution: Routh array: check sign changes in first column. No sign change = stable.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: RLC at f=258.90Hz (above fr=225.10Hz). Circuit behavior:
questions.solution: At f>fr, XL > XC. Phase angle positive. Power factor lagging.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Co-energy:
options: Energy in magnetic field W = ½Li²
questions.solution: Co-energy: W' = ∫φ·di. For linear system: W' = W = ½Li².

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The energy stored in a 1000 µF capacitor charged to 100 V is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Hunting in synchronous motor:
questions.solution: Caused by sudden load changes. Damper windings provide damping torque.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Chopper duty cycle D:
questions.solution: Buck converter: D = T_on/T. Boost: V_out = V_in/(1-D).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A diode conducts when it is:
questions.solution: A diode conducts in forward bias and blocks in reverse bias (ideally).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: SCR firing angle α:
options: Controls DC output voltage. V_avg = V_m(1+cosα)/2π
questions.solution: α=0°: max output. α=180°: zero output. Phase control principle.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: IGBT advantages:
questions.solution: IGBT: voltage controlled, fast switching, high current. Used in motor drives.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: RLC at f=35.60Hz (below fr=71.20Hz), R=47Ω. Circuit behavior:
questions.solution: At f<fr, XC > XL. Phase angle negative. Power factor leading.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A DC motor on 240 V draws 15 A with armature resistance 0.8 Ω. The back EMF is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Bode plot:
options: Magnitude (dB) and phase (°) vs log ω
questions.solution: Gain margin at -180° phase. Phase margin at 0dB gain. Stability margins.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Torque equation: T = (3V²·s·R₂)/((R₂)²+(sX₂)²)·ω_s
questions.solution: Crawling: harmonic torques. Cogging: magnetic locking.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 4-pole, 60 Hz induction motor runs at 1755 rpm. Its slip is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Core losses:
questions.solution: Hysteresis ∝ f·B_m^1.6. Eddy ∝ f²·B_m². Reduced by laminations.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The balanced Wheatstone bridge shown has P = 100 Ω, Q = 50 Ω and S = 40 Ω (galvanometer reads zero). The unknown resistance R is:
questions.solution: At balance P/Q = R/S, so R = P·S/Q = 100×40/50 = 80 Ω.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Ohm's law states that, at constant temperature, the current through a conductor is:
options: Proportional to the square of the voltage
Directly proportional to the voltage across it
questions.solution: V = IR, so for constant R the current rises in direct proportion to the applied voltage.
